    "Carbon Monoxide (CO) level is 292 ppm, which is extremely hazardous and far exceeds the OSHA permissible exposure limit (PEL) of 50 ppm and the action limit for safe entry."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Deny entry to the confined space immediately.",
    "Keep the space barricaded and post 'Do Not Enter' signs.",
    "Initiate mechanical ventilation and re-test the atmosphere once purged."
  ],
  "rationale": "The 4-gas monitor shows a Carbon Monoxide (CO) reading of 292 ppm. Under OSHA 1910.146, any atmospheric concentration of CO above the PEL (50 ppm) or action levels (typically 35 ppm or lower depending on company policy) is immediately dangerous and prohibits entry without appropriate supplied-air respirators and special authorization.",

    "OSHA 1910.146(c)(5)(i)(C)",
    "OSHA 1910.1000 Table Z-1"
  ]
